key_insight: FORA protects existing capabilities during fine-tuning by blocking updates in activation directions that those capabilities actually use, rather than relying only on weight-space geometry.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more targeted way to adapt large models while reducing catastrophic capability loss, useful when preserving prior skills matters as much as learning a new task.
method: Estimate per-layer activation principal directions from label-free calibration inputs.
Build a function-space projector to remove capability-relevant input directions, paired with a weight-space SVD projector.
Use an orthogonal residual update with a narrow spectral channel for controlled plasticity, and extend the same idea to PEFT.
evidence_signal: Across three Qwen3-1.7B settings, including translation-preservation and math-preservation tasks, FORA consistently improved capability retention over weight-space projection and standard regularization, with only a small new-task trade-off in the math-preservation case.
watch_item: Performance depends on representative calibration inputs, so poor calibration coverage could weaken the estimated capability subspace and reduce protection quality.
paper_title: Fora: From Weight-Space to Function-Space Protection in Capability-Preserving Fine-Tuning
